available_shells.title: Available shells
available_shells.why: Shells listed in /etc/shells, indicating available user environments.

backup_file_with_creds.title: Backup file contains credentials
backup_file_with_creds.why: Backup files may contain plaintext passwords, keys, or configuration secrets.
backup_file_with_creds.impact: Privilege escalation or lateral movement using recovered credentials.
backup_file_with_creds.recommendation: Secure, encrypt, or remove sensitive backup files and avoid storing plaintext secrets.

cap_net_raw.title: Binary with cap_net_raw capability
cap_net_raw.why: cap_net_raw allows crafting raw packets, enabling spoofing, scanning, or pivoting.
cap_net_raw.impact: Potential lateral movement, network attacks, or information gathering.
cap_net_raw.recommendation: Remove cap_net_raw unless strictly required for legitimate functionality.

capabilities-cap_dac_override.title: Binary with CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE capability
capabilities-cap_dac_override.why: CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE allows bypassing file read, write, and execute permission checks, enabling access to protected system files.
capabilities-cap_dac_override.impact: Potential full privilege escalation by modifying or reading sensitive files such as /etc/shadow, system binaries, or configuration paths.
capabilities-cap_dac_override.recommendation: Remove unnecessary capabilities using setcap -r, restrict access to the binary, and audit for unexpected capability assignments.

capabilities-cap_dac_read_search.title: DAC_READ_SEARCH capability assigned
capabilities-cap_dac_read_search.why: A process or container has the CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH capability.
capabilities-cap_dac_read_search.impact: This capability allows bypassing file read and directory search permissions, exposing sensitive data.
capabilities-cap_dac_read_search.recommendation: Remove CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH unless required. Audit processes with elevated file-read privileges. Apply least-privilege capability sets.

capabilities-cap_net_admin.title: NET_ADMIN capability assigned
capabilities-cap_net_admin.why: A process or container has the CAP_NET_ADMIN capability.
capabilities-cap_net_admin.impact: NET_ADMIN allows modification of network interfaces, routing, and firewall rules, increasing attack surface.
capabilities-cap_net_admin.recommendation: Remove CAP_NET_ADMIN unless strictly required. Use capability-dropping profiles. Audit processes with elevated network privileges.

capabilities-cap_setgid-cap_setuid.title: Binary with cap_setuid and cap_setgid
capabilities-cap_setgid-cap_setuid.why: These capabilities allow privilege manipulation similar to SUID binaries.
capabilities-cap_setgid-cap_setuid.recommendation: Remove these capabilities unless absolutely required.

capabilities-cap_sys_ptrace.title: cap_sys_ptrace capability present
capabilities-cap_sys_ptrace.why: cap_sys_ptrace allows tracing and modifying other processes, including those running as root.
capabilities-cap_sys_ptrace.impact: Attackers may read sensitive memory, inject code, or escalate privileges by attaching to privileged processes.
capabilities-cap_sys_ptrace.recommendation: Remove the capability unless absolutely required and restrict ptrace functionality via Yama or seccomp.

capabilities-present.title: Binary with Linux capabilities
capabilities-present.why: Binary has one or more Linux capabilities set.
capabilities-present.recommendation: Audit capabilities and remove unnecessary ones.

container-docker-sock.title: Docker socket exposed
container-docker-sock.why: /var/run/docker.sock is accessible, allowing control of the Docker daemon.
container-docker-sock.impact: Full host compromise via container abuse.
container-docker-sock.recommendation: Restrict access to docker.sock and avoid mounting it into containers.

container-host-mount.title: Container host filesystem mount
container-host-mount.why: A container is configured with a mount that exposes host filesystem paths.
container-host-mount.impact: Host mounts may allow modification of host files from inside the container, potentially enabling full host compromise.
container-host-mount.recommendation: Remove unnecessary host mounts. Use read-only mounts where possible. Avoid exposing sensitive directories.

container-lxc.title: LXC container environment detected
container-lxc.why: LXC containers may expose breakout vectors if misconfigured, especially when combined with writable host mounts or relaxed namespace restrictions.
container-lxc.impact: Potential escalation to host if the container is privileged, has writable host paths, or exposes unsafe capabilities.
container-lxc.recommendation: Verify whether the container is privileged, audit mounts and capabilities, and enforce unprivileged LXC profiles with strict isolation.

container-lxc-lxd.title: Privileged LXC/LXD container configuration
container-lxc-lxd.why: Privileged or improperly isolated LXC/LXD containers can grant access to host namespaces, device nodes, or filesystem paths, enabling breakout to the host.
container-lxc-lxd.impact: Potential full host compromise by mounting host filesystems, injecting device nodes, or manipulating container profiles.
container-lxc-lxd.recommendation: Review container profiles, remove privileged flags, restrict device access, and enforce unprivileged LXC/LXD configurations.

container-mount.title: Container mount configuration
container-mount.why: A container includes additional filesystem mounts that may expose sensitive data or increase attack surface.
container-mount.impact: Improperly scoped mounts may leak host information or allow unintended file access.
container-mount.recommendation: Review mount necessity and scope. Ensure mounts are read-only unless required. Avoid exposing host system directories.

container-privileged.title: Privileged container detected
container-privileged.why: Privileged containers share many capabilities with the host and can often escape confinement.
container-privileged.impact: Host compromise from within the container.
container-privileged.recommendation: Avoid privileged containers and use strict isolation.

container-runc.title: Container runtime exposure (runc)
container-runc.why: The runc binary is exposed or misconfigured in a way that may allow container breakout under certain conditions.
container-runc.impact: Misconfigured container runtimes may allow privilege escalation from container to host.
container-runc.recommendation: Ensure runc is root-owned and not writable. Avoid privileged containers. Review runtime configuration for unsafe flags.

cred-aws.title: AWS credentials exposed
cred-aws.why: AWS access key material was found in environment or files.
cred-aws.impact: Cloud account compromise, data theft, or infrastructure abuse.
cred-aws.recommendation: Revoke exposed keys, rotate credentials, and audit cloud activity.

cred-generic.title: Generic credential exposure
cred-generic.why: Environment variables, configuration files, and scripts often contain API keys, tokens, or passwords. Exposure allows attackers to authenticate to external services or internal systems.
cred-generic.impact: Potential compromise of cloud accounts, databases, internal APIs, or third‑party services depending on the credential type. May lead to lateral movement or full account takeover.
cred-generic.recommendation: Remove the exposed credential immediately, rotate the affected key or password, and audit logs for misuse. Store secrets in a dedicated secrets manager rather than environment variables or plaintext files.

cred-git.title: Git credential exposure
cred-git.why: Git configuration or repository metadata contains stored credentials or tokens.
cred-git.impact: Exposed Git credentials may allow access to private repositories, CI/CD systems, or internal tooling.
cred-git.recommendation: Remove credentials from Git config and history. Rotate any exposed tokens. Use credential helpers or environment-based authentication.

cred-password.title: Potential plaintext password exposure
cred-password.why: A value resembling a password was found in files, output, or configuration.
cred-password.impact: Privilege escalation or lateral movement using recovered credentials.
cred-password.recommendation: Remove plaintext passwords and use secure secret storage.

cred-private-key.title: Private key material exposed
cred-private-key.why: Private SSH keys grant authentication without passwords. Exposure allows attackers to impersonate the user or escalate privileges depending on authorized_keys configuration.
cred-private-key.impact: Full account compromise wherever the key is trusted. If the key belongs to root or a privileged service account, this may result in complete system takeover.
cred-private-key.recommendation: Remove the exposed key immediately, rotate all associated key pairs, and audit authorized_keys entries. Ensure private keys are stored with strict permissions and never embedded in scripts or environment variables.

cred-token.title: Potential token or secret exposure
cred-token.why: A value resembling an API token or secret was found.
cred-token.impact: Unauthorized access to services or data using exposed tokens.
cred-token.recommendation: Revoke exposed tokens and rotate credentials.

cron_jobs.title: Cron jobs
cron_jobs.why: System and user cron entries showing scheduled tasks and automation routines.

cron_path_hijack.title: Cron PATH hijack
cron_path_hijack.why: Cron jobs using relative commands and insecure PATH can execute attacker-controlled binaries.
cron_path_hijack.impact: Privilege escalation when cron runs as root or a privileged user.
cron_path_hijack.recommendation: Use absolute paths in cron jobs and avoid relying on PATH.

cron_writable_directory.title: Writable cron directory
cron_writable_directory.why: Writable cron directories allow attackers to drop new scripts executed by cron.
cron_writable_directory.impact: Full root compromise or persistent execution of malicious code.
cron_writable_directory.recommendation: Restrict permissions on cron directories and monitor for unauthorized files.

cron_writable_script.title: Writable cron script
cron_writable_script.why: Writable cron scripts allow attackers to inject commands executed automatically with elevated privileges.
cron_writable_script.impact: Full root compromise or persistent code execution.
cron_writable_script.recommendation: Restrict write permissions on cron scripts and directories.

cve-cve-2011-1485.title: Kernel memory leak (CVE-2011-1485)
cve-cve-2011-1485.why: A flaw in the kernel allows leaking uninitialised memory to user space.
cve-cve-2011-1485.impact: Information disclosure that may aid further attacks.
cve-cve-2011-1485.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2014-0038.title: futex privilege escalation (CVE-2014-0038)
cve-cve-2014-0038.why: Incorrect futex requeue handling allows memory corruption.
cve-cve-2014-0038.recommendation: Apply kernel patches addressing futex requeue logic.

cve-cve-2014-0196.title: tty race condition privilege escalation (CVE-2014-0196)
cve-cve-2014-0196.why: A race condition in the tty layer allows memory corruption.
cve-cve-2014-0196.impact: Local users may escalate privileges to root.
cve-cve-2014-0196.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2014-4014.title: ptrace permission bypass (CVE-2014-4014)
cve-cve-2014-4014.why: Incorrect ptrace permission checks allow bypassing security restrictions.
cve-cve-2014-4014.impact: Local attackers may escalate privileges or manipulate processes.
cve-cve-2014-4014.recommendation: Apply kernel patches improving ptrace permission validation.

cve-cve-2014-5207.title: Kernel memory corruption (CVE-2014-5207)
cve-cve-2014-5207.why: A flaw in the kernel's handling of certain memory operations allows corruption.
cve-cve-2014-5207.impact: Local attackers may escalate privileges or cause system instability.
cve-cve-2014-5207.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2014-9322.title: Kernel privilege escalation (CVE-2014-9322)
cve-cve-2014-9322.why: A flaw in the x86 emulation of SYSRET allows privilege escalation.
cve-cve-2014-9322.impact: Local attackers may escalate privileges on certain kernel builds.
cve-cve-2014-9322.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2015-1318.title: OverlayFS privilege escalation (CVE-2015-1318)
cve-cve-2015-1318.why: OverlayFS improperly validates permissions when copying files between layers.
cve-cve-2015-1318.impact: Local users may gain elevated privileges on affected systems.
cve-cve-2015-1318.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel or apply vendor fixes.

cve-cve-2015-1328.title: OverlayFS privilege escalation (CVE-2015-1328)
cve-cve-2015-1328.why: OverlayFS improperly handles permission checks when copying files between layers.
cve-cve-2015-1328.impact: Local users may gain elevated privileges on affected Ubuntu-based systems.
cve-cve-2015-1328.recommendation: Apply vendor patches or upgrade to a fixed kernel.

cve-cve-2015-3202.title: glibc nss_dns buffer overflow (CVE-2015-3202)
cve-cve-2015-3202.why: A stack-based buffer overflow occurs when handling long DNS responses.
cve-cve-2015-3202.impact: Potential code execution or privilege escalation depending on configuration.
cve-cve-2015-3202.recommendation: Upgrade glibc to a patched version.

cve-cve-2015-3290.title: Keyrings reference leak (CVE-2015-3290)
cve-cve-2015-3290.why: Incorrect reference counting in keyrings leads to memory corruption.
cve-cve-2015-3290.impact: Local users may escalate privileges or crash the kernel.
cve-cve-2015-3290.recommendation: Apply kernel patches addressing keyring reference handling.

cve-cve-2015-8660.title: Keyring memory corruption (CVE-2015-8660)
cve-cve-2015-8660.why: Incorrect handling of keyring reference counts can lead to memory corruption.
cve-cve-2015-8660.impact: Local attackers may escalate privileges or cause kernel crashes.
cve-cve-2015-8660.recommendation: Apply kernel patches addressing keyring reference handling.

cve-cve-2015-9322.title: jQuery prototype pollution (CVE-2015-9322)
cve-cve-2015-9322.why: Improper input sanitisation allows prototype pollution in older jQuery versions.
cve-cve-2015-9322.impact: May enable unexpected behaviour or injection depending on application context.
cve-cve-2015-9322.recommendation: Upgrade jQuery to a secure version.

cve-cve-2016-0728.title: Keyring reference leak (CVE-2016-0728)
cve-cve-2016-0728.why: A reference leak in keyrings allows attackers to trigger memory corruption.
cve-cve-2016-0728.impact: Local users may escalate privileges or crash the kernel.
cve-cve-2016-0728.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2016-2384.title: USB MIDI driver memory corruption (CVE-2016-2384)
cve-cve-2016-2384.why: A flaw in the USB MIDI driver allows attackers to trigger memory corruption via crafted descriptors.
cve-cve-2016-2384.impact: Local users may escalate privileges or crash the system.
cve-cve-2016-2384.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2016-5195.title: Dirty COW privilege escalation (CVE-2016-5195)
cve-cve-2016-5195.why: A race condition in the copy-on-write mechanism allows unprivileged users to modify read-only memory mappings.
cve-cve-2016-5195.impact: Local users can escalate privileges to root on all vulnerable kernels.
cve-cve-2016-5195.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version immediately.

cve-cve-2016-9793.title: perf subsystem privilege escalation (CVE-2016-9793)
cve-cve-2016-9793.why: Incorrect handling of perf_event_open leads to privilege escalation.
cve-cve-2016-9793.impact: Local users may gain elevated privileges.
cve-cve-2016-9793.recommendation: Apply kernel updates that fix perf event handling.

cve-cve-2017-6074.title: DCCP double-free vulnerability (CVE-2017-6074)
cve-cve-2017-6074.why: A double-free in the DCCP protocol implementation allows memory corruption.
cve-cve-2017-6074.impact: Local users may escalate privileges or potentially achieve remote code execution.
cve-cve-2017-6074.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2017-7308.title: AF_PACKET heap overflow (CVE-2017-7308)
cve-cve-2017-7308.why: A heap overflow in packet_set_ring allows controlled memory corruption.
cve-cve-2017-7308.impact: Local attackers can escalate privileges to root.

cve-cve-2017-1000253.title: ALSA race condition privilege escalation (CVE-2017-1000253)
cve-cve-2017-1000253.why: A race condition in ALSA PCM handling allows memory corruption.
cve-cve-2017-1000253.impact: Local attackers may escalate privileges to root.
cve-cve-2017-1000253.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2017-1000366.title: Stack guard-page bypass (CVE-2017-1000366)
cve-cve-2017-1000366.why: Improper stack expansion handling allows attackers to jump over the guard page.
cve-cve-2017-1000366.impact: Local users may escalate privileges by manipulating stack memory layout.
cve-cve-2017-1000366.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2017-1000379.title: Stack exhaustion privilege escalation (CVE-2017-1000379)
cve-cve-2017-1000379.why: Incorrect stack depth tracking allows attackers to force kernel stack exhaustion.
cve-cve-2017-1000379.impact: Local users may escalate privileges or crash the system.
cve-cve-2017-1000379.recommendation: Apply kernel patches addressing stack depth validation.

cve-cve-2018-1000001.title: glibc realpath buffer overflow (CVE-2018-1000001)
cve-cve-2018-1000001.why: A buffer overflow in realpath() can be triggered with long paths.
cve-cve-2018-1000001.impact: Potential privilege escalation depending on application context.
cve-cve-2018-1000001.recommendation: Update glibc to a patched version.

cve-cve-2019-13272.title: ptrace exploit privilege escalation (CVE-2019-13272)
cve-cve-2019-13272.why: A flaw in ptrace handling allows attackers to manipulate privileged processes.
cve-cve-2019-13272.impact: Local users may escalate privileges to root.
cve-cve-2019-13272.recommendation: Apply kernel patches addressing ptrace credential handling.

cve-cve-2019-15666.title: Netfilter memory corruption (CVE-2019-15666)
cve-cve-2019-15666.why: A flaw in Netfilter allows memory corruption via crafted rules.
cve-cve-2019-15666.impact: Local attackers may escalate privileges or crash the system.
cve-cve-2019-15666.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2019-18634.title: sudo pwfeedback buffer overflow (CVE-2019-18634)
cve-cve-2019-18634.why: A buffer overflow in sudo's pwfeedback feature allows memory corruption.
cve-cve-2019-18634.impact: Local users may escalate privileges to root.

cve-cve-2021-3156.title: Baron Samedit sudo heap overflow (CVE-2021-3156)
cve-cve-2021-3156.why: A heap-based buffer overflow in sudo allows bypassing security policies.
cve-cve-2021-3156.impact: Local users can gain root privileges even without sudo permissions.
cve-cve-2021-3156.recommendation: Upgrade sudo to a patched version.

cve-cve-2021-4034.title: PwnKit Polkit pkexec vulnerability (CVE-2021-4034)
cve-cve-2021-4034.why: A memory corruption flaw in pkexec allows execution of arbitrary commands as root.
cve-cve-2021-4034.impact: Unauthenticated local users can escalate privileges to root on default installations.
cve-cve-2021-4034.recommendation: Update Polkit to a patched version immediately.

cve-cve-2021-22555.title: Netfilter heap out-of-bounds write (CVE-2021-22555)
cve-cve-2021-22555.why: A heap buffer overflow in Netfilter allows attackers to corrupt kernel memory.
cve-cve-2021-22555.impact: Reliable privilege escalation and container escape.
cve-cve-2021-22555.recommendation: Apply kernel updates as soon as possible.

cve-cve-2022-0847.title: Dirty Pipe vulnerability detected (CVE-2022-0847)
cve-cve-2022-0847.why: Dirty Pipe allows overwriting read-only files and escalating privileges.
cve-cve-2022-0847.impact: Immediate privilege escalation to root on vulnerable kernels.
cve-cve-2022-0847.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel version.

cve-cve-2022-32250.title: Netfilter use-after-free (CVE-2022-32250)
cve-cve-2022-32250.why: A use-after-free in Netfilter allows controlled memory corruption.
cve-cve-2022-32250.impact: Local attackers can escalate privileges or escape containers.
cve-cve-2022-32250.recommendation: Update to a patched kernel immediately.

dangerous_capability.title: Binary with dangerous Linux capabilities
dangerous_capability.why: Capabilities like cap_setuid, cap_sys_admin, or cap_dac_override allow powerful privilege manipulation.
dangerous_capability.impact: Direct or near-direct privilege escalation to root or full system compromise.
dangerous_capability.recommendation: Remove unnecessary capabilities or replace the binary with a safer alternative.

db_creds_exposed.title: Database credentials exposed
db_creds_exposed.why: Database credentials in config files or logs allow direct access to sensitive data.
db_creds_exposed.impact: Data compromise, lateral movement, or privilege escalation via database abuse.
db_creds_exposed.recommendation: Secure configuration files, rotate credentials, and avoid plaintext storage.

docker_group_member.title: User in docker group
docker_group_member.why: Docker group membership effectively grants root-level access by allowing mounting of the host filesystem.
docker_group_member.impact: Full host compromise via container escape or direct filesystem access.
docker_group_member.recommendation: Restrict docker group membership to trusted administrators only.

env-ld-library-path.title: Unsafe LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable
env-ld-library-path.why: LD_LIBRARY_PATH alters the dynamic linker search order. If it includes writable or untrusted directories, attackers can place malicious libraries that override legitimate system libraries.
env-ld-library-path.impact: Execution of attacker‑controlled shared libraries when binaries are launched. If inherited by privileged processes, this can lead to privilege escalation or arbitrary code execution.
env-ld-library-path.recommendation: Remove writable or untrusted directories from LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Ensure privileged services run with a sanitized environment. Use absolute library paths or rpath/runpath where appropriate.

env-ld-preload.title: Dangerous LD_PRELOAD environment variable
env-ld-preload.why: LD_PRELOAD forces the dynamic linker to load an arbitrary shared library before any others. If set in a privileged context, it allows attackers to inject malicious code into any executed binary.
env-ld-preload.impact: Potential full compromise of any process that inherits this variable. If a privileged process runs with LD_PRELOAD set, it can lead to immediate root-level code execution.
env-ld-preload.recommendation: Unset LD_PRELOAD immediately and investigate how it was introduced. Prevent untrusted users from influencing environment variables for privileged services. Use secure wrapper scripts or systemd units that sanitize the environment.

env-shell-override.title: Environment variable shell override
env-shell-override.why: Environment variables such as SHELL, EDITOR, or PAGER may override default system utilities.
env-shell-override.impact: If privileged processes inherit untrusted environment variables, users may influence which binaries are executed.
env-shell-override.recommendation: Sanitise environment variables in privileged scripts and services. Use absolute paths in systemd units and cron jobs.

env-suspicious.title: Suspicious environment variable
env-suspicious.why: Environment variables with names or values resembling secrets, tokens, or execution‑influencing settings may indicate credential exposure or unsafe runtime manipulation.
env-suspicious.impact: Potential leakage of sensitive information or unintended influence over program behavior. If inherited by privileged processes, this may enable credential theft or execution of attacker‑controlled code.
env-suspicious.recommendation: Review and sanitize environment variables. Remove sensitive values, rotate any exposed credentials, and ensure privileged services run with a minimal, controlled environment.

env-path-writable.title: Writable directory in PATH
env-path-writable.why: Writable directories in PATH allow attackers to place malicious binaries that override legitimate ones.
env-path-writable.impact: Privilege escalation when privileged scripts rely on PATH resolution.
env-path-writable.recommendation: Remove writable directories from PATH and use absolute paths in privileged scripts.

env_vars.title: Environment variables
env_vars.why: System and user environment variables collected to understand runtime configuration.

exec-cron-job.title: Executable cron job
exec-cron-job.why: A cron job executes a script or binary that may be writable or influenced by non-privileged users.
exec-cron-job.impact: Writable cron scripts or paths may allow users to influence privileged execution flow.
exec-cron-job.recommendation: Ensure cron scripts and directories are root-owned and non-writable. Use absolute paths. Audit for world-writable directories.

exec-interpreter-writable.title: Writable interpreter or privileged execution dependency
exec-interpreter-writable.why: If an interpreter or file used by privileged scripts is writable, an attacker can hijack execution and run arbitrary commands as root.
exec-interpreter-writable.impact: Complete privilege escalation by replacing or modifying the interpreter or privileged input file.
exec-interpreter-writable.recommendation: Ensure interpreters and privileged execution paths are owned by root and not writable by unprivileged users; restore secure permissions immediately.

exec-missing-binary.title: Missing binary in execution flow
exec-missing-binary.why: A referenced binary does not exist, potentially allowing hijacking by placing a malicious binary in its place.
exec-missing-binary.impact: Privilege escalation or arbitrary code execution if a privileged service calls the missing binary.
exec-missing-binary.recommendation: Fix service definitions and ensure all referenced binaries exist and are secured.

exec-service.title: Executable systemd service
exec-service.why: A systemd service executes a script or binary that may be writable or misconfigured.
exec-service.impact: Writable service files or execution paths may allow modification of privileged service behaviour.
exec-service.recommendation: Ensure service files and binaries are root-owned and immutable. Avoid relative paths. Audit service directories for writable locations.

exec-service-writable.title: Writable systemd service unit
exec-service-writable.why: Writable systemd service files allow modification of ExecStart or related directives, enabling arbitrary command execution with root privileges.
exec-service-writable.impact: Full system compromise by injecting commands into a service executed by systemd as root.
exec-service-writable.recommendation: Restore root ownership and secure permissions on service files, reload systemd, and audit for unauthorized modifications.

exec-writable-exec-path.title: Writable path in service or execution flow
exec-writable-exec-path.why: Writable systemd service files, cron scripts, or execution paths allow attackers to modify commands executed with elevated privileges. Any user able to write to these files can inject arbitrary commands.
exec-writable-exec-path.impact: Immediate privilege escalation. A modified service file or script will run attacker‑controlled code as root on the next service start, reload, or system reboot.
exec-writable-exec-path.recommendation: Correct file permissions so only root can modify service units and execution paths. Audit all writable directories and files referenced by systemd services, cron jobs, and startup scripts. Reload or restart services only after verifying integrity.

groups_list.title: Group memberships
groups_list.why: Local groups and their associated users, providing insight into system roles and privilege boundaries.

hostname_info.title: Hostname and domain information
hostname_info.why: System hostname and domain membership information collected for situational awareness.

installed_packages.title: Installed packages
installed_packages.why: List of installed packages from the system package manager, showing software footprint.

json-block-unclassified.title: Unclassified JSON‑shaped block
json-block-unclassified.why: A JSON‑shaped block was detected but does not match any known credential, configuration, or security‑relevant patterns.
json-block-unclassified.impact: Likely minimal. However, JSON‑shaped blocks can sometimes contain embedded secrets, misconfigurations, or partial data structures that require manual review.
json-block-unclassified.recommendation: Inspect the block to confirm it does not contain credentials, tokens, or sensitive configuration. If a new pattern is identified, update LPA’s JSON analysis rules to classify it correctly.

kernel_exploit_possible.title: Kernel version vulnerable to known exploits
kernel_exploit_possible.why: Outdated kernels may contain publicly known privilege escalation vulnerabilities.
kernel_exploit_possible.impact: Immediate root compromise using known kernel exploits.
kernel_exploit_possible.recommendation: Update the kernel to a supported, patched version.

kernel_modules_writable.title: Writable kernel module directory
kernel_modules_writable.why: Writable module directories allow attackers to load malicious kernel modules.
kernel_modules_writable.impact: Full system compromise at the kernel level.
kernel_modules_writable.recommendation: Restrict write access to kernel module directories and enforce module signing.

kvm_group_member.title: User in kvm group
kvm_group_member.why: Access to /dev/kvm can enable advanced attacks or VM escape techniques.
kvm_group_member.impact: Potential privilege escalation or host compromise via hypervisor abuse.
kvm_group_member.recommendation: Restrict kvm group membership to trusted virtualization administrators.

ld_library_path_hijack.title: LD_LIBRARY_PATH hijack
ld_library_path_hijack.why: Manipulating library search paths can cause privileged binaries to load malicious shared objects.
ld_library_path_hijack.impact: Privilege escalation via arbitrary code execution in privileged processes.
ld_library_path_hijack.recommendation: Avoid using LD_LIBRARY_PATH in privileged contexts and use absolute library paths.

ld_preload_injection.title: LD_PRELOAD injection opportunity
ld_preload_injection.why: LD_PRELOAD can force privileged binaries to load attacker-controlled shared libraries.
ld_preload_injection.impact: Immediate code execution in the context of a privileged process, often root.
ld_preload_injection.recommendation: Unset LD_PRELOAD for privileged binaries and avoid honoring it in setuid programs.

libvirt_group_member.title: User in libvirt group
libvirt_group_member.why: Libvirt group members can control VMs and potentially interact with the host.
libvirt_group_member.impact: Lateral movement or privilege escalation via VM management.
libvirt_group_member.recommendation: Restrict libvirt group membership and monitor VM configurations.

listening_ports.title: Listening ports
listening_ports.why: Open TCP/UDP ports and associated processes, mapping exposed services.

lxc-privileged-container.title: Privileged LXC container
lxc-privileged-container.why: Privileged containers share many capabilities with the host and can often escape confinement.
lxc-privileged-container.impact: Host compromise from within the container.
lxc-privileged-container.recommendation: Avoid privileged containers and use user namespaces and strict isolation.

mounted_filesystems.title: Mounted filesystems
mounted_filesystems.why: Mounted volumes and their options, showing storage layout and architecture.

network_info.title: Network configuration
network_info.why: Interfaces, IP addresses, and routing information collected for connectivity awareness.

nfs_root_squash_disabled.title: NFS root_squash disabled
nfs_root_squash_disabled.why: Without root_squash, remote root users can write files as root on the NFS server.
nfs_root_squash_disabled.impact: Full system compromise via remote file manipulation.
nfs_root_squash_disabled.recommendation: Enable root_squash on NFS exports and restrict client access.

nfs_writable_share.title: Writable NFS share
nfs_writable_share.why: Writable NFS shares allow attackers to drop or modify files on the server.
nfs_writable_share.impact: Privilege escalation or persistence via malicious files or scripts.
nfs_writable_share.recommendation: Restrict write access on NFS shares and enforce strong export options.

os_info.title: Operating system information
os_info.why: General OS metadata collected by linpeas, including distribution, version, kernel, and architecture.

passwd_writable.title: Writable /etc/passwd
passwd_writable.why: Writable /etc/passwd allows attackers to add or modify user entries, including root.
passwd_writable.impact: Immediate root compromise by adding a user with UID 0 or modifying existing entries.
passwd_writable.recommendation: Restrict write access to /etc/passwd to root-only.

password_in_env.title: Password found in environment variables
password_in_env.why: Environment variables can be read by processes or leaked via debugging tools.
password_in_env.impact: Privilege escalation or lateral movement using exposed credentials.
password_in_env.recommendation: Avoid storing secrets in environment variables or use secure secret management solutions.

password_in_history.title: Password found in shell history
password_in_history.why: Shell history may contain plaintext passwords or sensitive commands.
password_in_history.impact: Privilege escalation or lateral movement using recovered credentials.
password_in_history.recommendation: Clear shell history, avoid typing secrets directly, and configure shells to ignore sensitive commands.

password_in_script.title: Password hardcoded in script
password_in_script.why: Hardcoded credentials can be extracted by reading or decompiling scripts.
password_in_script.impact: Privilege escalation or unauthorized access to services and systems.
password_in_script.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials and use secure secret storage mechanisms.

path_entries.title: PATH variable contents
path_entries.why: Directories included in the system PATH, showing command resolution order.

path_hijack.title: Writable directory in PATH (PATH hijack opportunity)
path_hijack.why: Writable directories included in PATH allow attackers to place malicious executables that override legitimate system binaries. Any process relying on PATH-based resolution may execute attacker-controlled code, and if a privileged process inherits this PATH, it can lead to privilege escalation or full system compromise.
path_hijack.impact: Execution of malicious binaries when common commands are invoked. Privileged scripts or services inheriting an unsafe PATH may unintentionally run attacker-supplied executables, enabling privilege escalation or complete system takeover.
path_hijack.recommendation: Remove writable or untrusted directories from PATH. Use absolute paths in privileged scripts and ensure privileged services define a strict, sanitized PATH. Restrict write permissions on directories intended for executable files.

proc_mem_accessible.title: Readable /proc/<pid>/mem
proc_mem_accessible.why: Access to process memory can allow credential theft or code injection.
proc_mem_accessible.impact: Privilege escalation or data theft via direct memory manipulation.
proc_mem_accessible.recommendation: Restrict ptrace and memory access using kernel settings and security modules.

process_list.title: Running processes
process_list.why: Snapshot of active processes to understand system activity.

services_list.title: System services
services_list.why: List of systemd or init services and their states, providing operational context.

shadow_readable.title: Shadow file readable
shadow_readable.why: Reading /etc/shadow allows offline cracking of password hashes.
shadow_readable.impact: Full account compromise, including root, if passwords are cracked.

shadow_writable.title: Writable /etc/shadow
shadow_writable.why: Writable /etc/shadow allows attackers to set known password hashes for any user.
shadow_writable.impact: Immediate root or account compromise by setting a known password.
shadow_writable.recommendation: Restrict write access to /etc/shadow to root-only.

ssh_config_weak.title: Weak SSH configuration
ssh_config_weak.why: Weak SSH settings (e.g., password auth, root login) increase attack surface.
ssh_config_weak.impact: Higher likelihood of brute-force or credential-based compromise.
ssh_config_weak.recommendation: Harden SSH configuration (disable root login, use key-based auth, restrict ciphers).

ssh_key_exposed.title: Exposed SSH private key
ssh_key_exposed.why: Private SSH keys allow impersonation of users and access to remote systems.
ssh_key_exposed.impact: Full system compromise or lateral movement depending on key usage.
ssh_key_exposed.recommendation: Remove exposed keys, rotate credentials, and restrict file permissions.

sudo_edit_abuse.title: Sudoedit allows editing privileged files
sudo_edit_abuse.why: Misconfigured sudoedit rules can allow editing arbitrary files as root.
sudo_edit_abuse.impact: Privilege escalation via modification of configuration files, scripts, or authorized_keys.
sudo_edit_abuse.recommendation: Restrict sudoedit to specific, non-sensitive paths and validate configuration.

sudo_env_keep.title: Sudo preserves dangerous environment variables
sudo_env_keep.why: Preserving variables like LD_PRELOAD or LD_LIBRARY_PATH can allow code injection into privileged processes.
sudo_env_keep.impact: Privilege escalation by loading attacker-controlled libraries.
sudo_env_keep.recommendation: Remove dangerous environment variables from env_keep and use secure defaults.

sudo-find-nopasswd.title: NOPASSWD sudo entry for find
sudo-find-nopasswd.why: find supports executing arbitrary commands via -exec and -execdir, enabling privilege escalation when run with elevated permissions.
sudo-find-nopasswd.impact: Immediate root shell via command execution through find’s -exec functionality.
sudo-find-nopasswd.recommendation: Remove this rule or restrict find usage to tightly controlled, non-sensitive paths. Avoid granting NOPASSWD access to command-executing binaries.

sudo-nano-nopasswd.title: NOPASSWD sudo entry for nano
sudo-nano-nopasswd.why: nano allows shell escapes and file editing, making it dangerous under NOPASSWD.
sudo-nano-nopasswd.impact: Immediate root shell via shell escape.
sudo-nano-nopasswd.recommendation: Remove this rule or restrict nano usage.

sudo_nopasswd.title: NOPASSWD sudo entry
sudo_nopasswd.why: NOPASSWD allows execution of privileged commands without authentication.
sudo_nopasswd.impact: Full root compromise depending on the commands allowed.
sudo_nopasswd.recommendation: Remove NOPASSWD or restrict it to strictly controlled, non-sensitive commands.

sudo_shell_escape.title: Sudo command allows shell escape
sudo_shell_escape.why: Commands like vim, less, awk, find, and others can spawn interactive shells when run with sudo.
sudo_shell_escape.impact: Immediate root shell if the user can run such commands via sudo.
sudo_shell_escape.recommendation: Restrict sudo to non-interactive, non-shell-escaping binaries.

sudo-vim-nopasswd.title: NOPASSWD sudo entry for vim
sudo-vim-nopasswd.why: vim provides multiple shell escape mechanisms, allowing arbitrary command execution when run with elevated privileges.
sudo-vim-nopasswd.impact: Immediate root shell via :! commands, shell escapes, or editing privileged files.
sudo-vim-nopasswd.recommendation: Remove this rule or restrict vim usage to non-privileged contexts. Avoid granting NOPASSWD access to editors with shell escape capabilities.

sudo_wildcard.title: Wildcard sudo entry
sudo_wildcard.why: Wildcards in sudo rules can be abused to execute unintended binaries or shell escapes.
sudo_wildcard.impact: Privilege escalation via execution of arbitrary or unexpected commands as root.
sudo_wildcard.recommendation: Replace wildcards with explicit command paths and arguments.

suid_custom_binary.title: Custom or unusual SUID binary
suid_custom_binary.why: Custom SUID binaries may contain insecure logic, unsafe functions, or insufficient input validation.
suid_custom_binary.impact: Potential privilege escalation if the binary can be abused or exploited.
suid_custom_binary.recommendation: Review custom SUID binaries for insecure operations and remove SUID where not strictly required.

suid-node.title: SUID Node.js binary
suid-node.why: Node.js is installed with the SUID bit set, allowing JavaScript execution with elevated privileges.
suid-node.impact: SUID interpreters can run arbitrary code with elevated privileges, representing a severe privilege boundary risk.
suid-node.recommendation: Immediately remove the SUID bit. Investigate why Node was configured this way and audit for other SUID interpreters.

suid_nonroot.title: SUID binary owned by non-root user
suid_nonroot.why: If a SUID binary is owned by a non-root user, that user can replace or modify it.
suid_nonroot.impact: Privilege escalation to root by replacing or modifying the SUID binary.
suid_nonroot.recommendation: Restrict ownership of SUID binaries to root and regularly audit SUID files.

suid-vi.title: SUID text editor present
suid-vi.why: A text editor such as vi/vim has the SUID bit set, allowing execution with elevated privileges.
suid-vi.impact: SUID editors may allow modification of privileged files or execution of unintended commands with elevated rights.
suid-vi.recommendation: Remove the SUID bit from vi/vim. Audit SUID binaries and ensure only essential ones remain.

suid_writable.title: Writable SUID binary
suid_writable.why: Writable SUID binaries allow attackers to replace the binary with arbitrary code executed as the file owner.
suid_writable.impact: Immediate privilege escalation to the owner of the SUID binary, often root.
suid_writable.recommendation: Remove the SUID bit or restrict write permissions to prevent modification.

system_architecture.title: System architecture
system_architecture.why: CPU architecture extracted from uname output.

system_hostname.title: System hostname disclosure
system_hostname.why: The hostname is visible in multiple system locations. This is normal but may reveal naming conventions or internal topology.
system_hostname.impact: Hostnames can provide contextual clues about environment roles (e.g., prod, db, backup) which may support reconnaissance.
system_hostname.recommendation: Ensure hostnames do not reveal sensitive roles or internal structure. Review naming conventions for unnecessary information leakage.

system_kernel_version.title: Kernel version information
system_kernel_version.why: Kernel version extracted from uname output.
system_kernel_version.impact: Older kernels may contain known vulnerabilities.
system_kernel_version.recommendation: Ensure the kernel is up to date with vendor patches.

system_os.title: Operating system detected
system_os.why: Distribution and OS version extracted from system metadata.

system_uname_info.title: System uname information
system_uname_info.why: General uname output for system identification.

systemd_path_hijack.title: Systemd PATH hijack
systemd_path_hijack.why: Systemd services using relative commands and insecure PATH can execute attacker-controlled binaries.
systemd_path_hijack.impact: Privilege escalation when services run as root.
systemd_path_hijack.recommendation: Use absolute paths in systemd unit files and avoid writable directories in PATH.

systemd_unit_writable.title: Writable systemd unit file
systemd_unit_writable.why: Writable unit files allow attackers to configure arbitrary commands to run as root.
systemd_unit_writable.impact: Immediate root compromise and persistent execution.
systemd_unit_writable.recommendation: Restrict write access to systemd unit files and reload only trusted configurations.

token-aws_access_key.title: Exposed AWS Access Key
token-aws_access_key.why: AWS access keys allow programmatic access to AWS APIs. If compromised, attackers may enumerate resources, exfiltrate data, or escalate privileges.
token-aws_access_key.impact: Potential full cloud account compromise depending on IAM permissions.
token-aws_access_key.recommendation: Immediately disable and rotate the key in IAM. Review CloudTrail logs for suspicious activity. Enforce IAM least privilege and use IAM roles instead of static keys.

token-aws_secret_key.title: AWS Secret Access Key exposed
token-aws_secret_key.why: The AWS Secret Access Key provides full authentication capability when paired with an Access Key ID, enabling complete AWS API access.
token-aws_secret_key.impact: Full compromise of AWS resources including data exfiltration, privilege escalation, and destructive operations depending on IAM policy.
token-aws_secret_key.recommendation: Immediately revoke and rotate the exposed key, investigate misuse, and implement secure secret storage mechanisms.

token-detected.title: Exposed Token or Secret
token-detected.why: A token or secret was detected in the system. These values often grant direct access to APIs, cloud services, version control systems, or authentication mechanisms.
token-detected.impact: An attacker with access to this token may authenticate as the associated user or service, potentially leading to data exfiltration, privilege escalation, or full system compromise.
token-detected.recommendation: Immediately revoke the exposed token, rotate associated credentials, and remove the token from the filesystem or environment. Implement secret-scanning and enforce least-privilege token scopes.

token-generic_api_key.title: Generic API key exposed
token-generic_api_key.why: API keys found in files or environment variables may grant access to third‑party services or internal APIs.
token-generic_api_key.impact: Potential unauthorized access to APIs, data leakage, or service abuse depending on the permissions associated with the key.
token-generic_api_key.recommendation: Rotate the exposed key, remove it from the filesystem, and migrate to secure secret management solutions.

token-github.title: Exposed GitHub Personal Access Token
token-github.why: GitHub tokens can grant access to private repositories, commit history, secrets, and CI/CD pipelines.
token-github.impact: An attacker may clone private repositories, inject malicious code, access deployment secrets, or pivot into connected systems.
token-github.recommendation: Revoke the token immediately via GitHub settings. Rotate any secrets stored in affected repositories. Enable secret scanning and enforce fine-grained PAT scopes.

token-json-block.title: JSON block containing potential secrets
token-json-block.why: JSON structures may contain embedded tokens, passwords, or API keys that are not immediately visible in plaintext scanning.
token-json-block.impact: Exposure of sensitive configuration data or authentication material that could enable unauthorized access.
token-json-block.recommendation: Inspect the JSON block for secrets, remove hardcoded credentials, and migrate to secure secret storage.

token-jwt.title: Exposed JSON Web Token
token-jwt.why: JWTs often represent authenticated sessions or API access tokens.
token-jwt.impact: An attacker may impersonate the user or service associated with the token until it expires.
token-jwt.recommendation: Invalidate the token server-side if possible. Reduce token lifetime, enforce refresh tokens, and avoid storing JWTs in world-readable locations.

unpatched_cve.title: Unpatched CVE detected
unpatched_cve.why: Known vulnerabilities may be exploitable locally or remotely depending on the CVE.
unpatched_cve.impact: Privilege escalation, data compromise, or full system takeover.
unpatched_cve.recommendation: Apply vendor security patches and track CVE remediation.

unrestricted_dmesg.title: Unrestricted dmesg access
unrestricted_dmesg.why: Unrestricted dmesg may leak sensitive kernel or memory information.
unrestricted_dmesg.impact: Information disclosure that may aid in exploit development.

uptime_info.title: System uptime
uptime_info.why: Duration since the last system boot, useful for understanding system stability and maintenance cycles.

users_list.title: User accounts
users_list.why: List of local users discovered on the system for contextual awareness.

weak_file_permissions.title: Weak file permissions
weak_file_permissions.why: Overly permissive file permissions allow unauthorized modification or reading.
weak_file_permissions.impact: Privilege escalation, data leakage, or tampering.
weak_file_permissions.recommendation: Apply least privilege to file permissions and regularly audit them.

weak_service_permissions.title: Weak service file permissions
weak_service_permissions.why: Writable service files allow attackers to modify startup behavior or executed commands.
weak_service_permissions.impact: Privilege escalation or persistent code execution as root.
weak_service_permissions.recommendation: Restrict write access to service files and directories.

weak_umask.title: Weak system umask
weak_umask.why: A weak umask may create files that are world-readable or writable.
weak_umask.impact: Data exposure or privilege escalation via writable files.
weak_umask.recommendation: Set a secure default umask (e.g., 027 or 077).

world_writable_directory.title: World-writable system directory
world_writable_directory.why: World-writable directories allow attackers to drop or replace files used by privileged processes.
world_writable_directory.impact: Privilege escalation or persistence via malicious files.
world_writable_directory.recommendation: Restrict directory permissions and avoid world-writable system paths.

world_writable_file.title: World-writable system file
world_writable_file.why: World-writable files can be modified by any user, enabling tampering or code injection.
world_writable_file.impact: Privilege escalation, data tampering, or persistence depending on the file's role.
world_writable_file.recommendation: Remove world-writable permissions from system files and apply least privilege.
